title: Feature Priority — Ordering and Importance Signalling
problem: Features render in insertion order with no semantic meaning — visitors can't tell which are foundational vs incidental, and tools can't sort siblings meaningfully.

lineage.spawnReason: Integration sprint exposed that features had no semantic ordering — priority is a first-class schema field, not a rendering concern.

analysis: Priority is an integer 1–5 (1 = highest) controlling sibling order in every rendering surface: CLI lineage tree, MCP get_lineage output, and web app tree view. Features without priority sort last (treated as 9999 internally). Priority is intentionally separate from completeness score: score measures documentation quality; priority measures sibling importance.

decisions.rationale: Integers sort deterministically with no ambiguity — an enum requires a sort-key mapping anyway and leaves same-level peers in undefined relative order.
alternativesConsidered: Enum — cleaner to read but non-deterministic sort
Float 0.0–1.0 — false precision, no human-scale meaning

decisions.decision: Sort: priority ASC NULLS LAST, then featureKey as tiebreaker
decisions.rationale: Unset features fall to the end rather than the front — opt-in semantics. featureKey is used as a deterministic tiebreaker (no updatedAt field in this static showcase).
alternativesConsidered: NULLS FIRST — forces all legacy features to the top, breaking existing trees
updatedAt tiebreaker — no such field exists in the static Vite/React app

decisions.decision: Add priority to feature-schema, db schema, validators, and all rendering surfaces atomically
decisions.rationale: A half-implemented field is worse than no field — schema, CLI, MCP, and web app must all agree on the same contract simultaneously.
alternativesConsidered: Incremental rollout — creates a window where the field exists but isn't used anywhere

implementation: In this Vite/React showcase, priority is a field on the Feature TypeScript interface (priority?: number) declared in types.ts. It is read directly from each feature.json at build time via import.meta.glob in features.ts — no DB or API involved. The visibleTree useMemo in App.tsx applies an optional sort: when sortBy === 'priority', result.sort() orders features by priority ASC (null treated as 999) then featureKey as a tiebreaker. Priority is also surfaced as a P-badge on each card (feat-2026-029) and is used by the LAC MCP roadmap_view tool to group and order features in the terminal output. The priority integer 1–5 in the feature-schema package and the lifeascode web app DB schema follows the same contract but is out of scope for this static site.
knownLimitations: Priority without bounds breaks sibling sort
No linting rule enforces priority range
successCriteria: Running lac lineage feat-2026-001 shows children in priority order, the web app tree renders siblings sorted by priority, and features without priority sort last.

statusHistory.reason: Implementation field describes the lifeascode web app (DB column, tRPC mutation, migration, build-tree.ts) — none of which exists in this Vite/React showcase. Need to describe actual implementation: priority is a field on Feature interface, read from feature.json, client-side sorted in visibleTree useMemo.

annotations.author: lac advance

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Previous implementation described lifeascode web app DB/tRPC/migration code which does not exist in this Vite/React static showcase. Rewrote to accurately describe how priority works here: Feature interface field, import.meta.glob read, client-side sort in visibleTree.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Fixed decisions: tiebreaker is featureKey (not updatedAt — updatedAt does not exist in this static showcase). Cleared stale-review annotation since implementation was already corrected.
